title: ranunculus montanus subsp. aurasiacus
intro: Ranunculus montanus subsp. aurasiacus, also known as Aurasiacus Buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family Ranunculaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of Ranunculus montanus subsp. aurasiacus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
